title: MyCR comment-only full sweep 2026-07-09 16:53
overview: 本轮从 mycr 仓库执行 git pull 后刷新 trpc-group/trpc-agent-go 的完整 open PR 列表。当前 open PR 为 59 个，其中 28 个 checks 全绿、31 个非绿或仍在 pending。本轮严格执行只发 comments：没有 LGTM、没有 approval、没有 merge，也没有对自己的 PR 做自审或合入。实际提交 COMMENT review 的 PR 为 #2121、#2128、#2136、#2141、#2151、#2152、#2157，共 14 条 inline comments；#2158 与 #2159 经深度复核未发现新阻塞，但按只评论策略不提交 LGTM。#2028 自上次报告后已关闭；新增 open PR 为 #2157、#2158、#2159、#2160。

commented.title: internal/telemetry:Reduce streaming TraceChat request attribute allocations
commented.author: cuboya

inline_comments.body:
The stateless path now builds a throwaway `chatRequestAttributes` when `requestAttrs` is nil, so ordinary `TraceChat` / `buildRequestAttributes` calls still populate `slot.attrs` and allocate cloned message attributes that are immediately discarded. Please keep the uncached request-attribute builder for the nil-state path, and reserve cache-slot population for a persistent `ChatTraceState`.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
无状态路径现在会在 `requestAttrs` 为 nil 时创建一个一次性的 `chatRequestAttributes`，导致普通 `TraceChat` / `buildRequestAttributes` 调用也会填充 `slot.attrs`，并分配马上被丢弃的 message 属性副本。建议 nil-state 路径保留未缓存的 request attribute 构造逻辑，只在持久的 `ChatTraceState` 中写入缓存槽。
</details>

commented.review_action: Submitted COMMENT review only; no approval, no LGTM, no merge. Review URL: https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2141#pullrequestreview-4661543244
commented.technical_background: Telemetry trace code turns chat requests and responses into OpenTelemetry span attributes. Streaming model calls may emit many chunks for the same request, so request-side attributes are a hot path where repeated JSON marshaling and attribute allocation can add avoidable latency and heap pressure.
commented.problem: The PR tries to reduce allocation churn while tracing streaming chat requests, especially in `llmflow`, where the request portion stays stable while response chunks change over time.
commented.necessity_assessment: confirmed: the touched path rebuilds request attributes repeatedly during streaming, and the PR adds reusable per-span request attribute state to reduce that cost.
commented.evidence_checked: PR body, changed telemetry code, checks, benchmark notes from the reviewer, and targeted telemetry/llmflow tests were inspected.
commented.problem_framing: The immediate problem is allocation pressure, but the underlying contract is whether `TraceChat` remains a cheap stateless helper while `ChatTraceState` owns reusable per-span cache state.
commented.approach: The PR adds `ChatTraceState`, moves reusable request fields into `chatRequestAttributes`, and lets streaming processors call the stateful tracing path so repeated request attributes can be reused across chunks.
commented.implementation_derivation: Putting reusable request attribute state next to the streaming chat span is the right locality for chunked responses; the issue is that the nil-state fallback also uses the cache-filling path.
commented.solution_fit_assessment: The stateful API fits streaming reuse, but the stateless fallback should stay uncached so ordinary one-shot calls do not pay cache bookkeeping or clone allocation costs.
commented.alternative_designs: Alternatives include keeping the old stateless builder for all callers, caching only serialized message bytes, or making the processor own a precomputed request attribute slice.
commented.tradeoffs: A reusable trace state reduces repeated work in streaming paths, but it adds cache invalidation and message-identity comparison complexity that should be limited to callers with persistent state.
commented.design_assessment: The design is directionally sound for streaming trace spans, with one localized regression in the nil-state path that should be fixed before the allocation claim is fully true.
commented.modules: internal/telemetry trace builders and internal/flow/llmflow streaming instrumentation.
commented.api_surface: Adds `ChatTraceState` as a reusable tracing helper; existing `TraceChat` remains the stateless public helper.
commented.change_inventory: Introduces reusable request attribute slots, message identity checks, stateful trace calls from llmflow, and benchmark/test coverage for cache behavior.
commented.semantic_changes: Streaming trace emission can reuse request attributes across chunks; stateless `TraceChat` behavior should remain equivalent but currently takes the cache population path.
commented.module_impact: Direct impact is telemetry allocation behavior; indirect impact is any caller relying on `TraceChat` as a cheap one-shot helper.
commented.cross_module_impact: llmflow benefits from lower repeated attribute work; OpenTelemetry export content should stay compatible.
commented.behavior_impact: Expected before/after: streaming spans carry the same request attributes, but repeated chunks allocate less when a persistent trace state is used.
commented.tests_docs: `go test ./internal/telemetry ./internal/flow/llmflow` and targeted telemetry tests were reported passing by the reviewer; benchmark inspection showed the cache reduces streaming allocations.
commented.outcome: Commented on the nil-state fallback because it still populates cache slots and clones attributes that are immediately discarded.
commented.risk: If unfixed, the non-stateful path can regress allocation behavior even while the streaming stateful path improves.

commented.title: evaluation/promptiter: add eval+optimization regression loop with aud…
commented.author: d2bz

inline_comments.body:
The CLI help still omits `attribution`, even though it is a supported scenario and part of the sample reports. Please add it to the `--scenario` flag text so `--help` matches the README and users can discover the attribution run.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
CLI 帮助文本仍然漏掉了 `attribution`，但它已经是受支持场景，也是示例报告的一部分。请把它加入 `--scenario` 的说明里，让 `--help` 与 README 保持一致，并方便用户发现 attribution 运行模式。
</details>

commented.review_action: Submitted COMMENT review only; no approval, no LGTM, no merge. Review URL: https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2157#pullrequestreview-4661543248
commented.technical_background: PromptIter examples demonstrate evaluation and optimization flows with deterministic fake runners, sample eval sets, metrics, and generated reports. The command-line flags are part of the example contract because users discover scenarios through `--help` before reading all fixtures.
commented.problem: The PR adds a reproducible evaluation to failure-attribution to PromptIter optimization loop, including multiple deterministic scenarios and committed sample reports.
commented.necessity_assessment: confirmed as a feature need: the linked maintainer task asks for auditability, failure attribution, validation regression, and release gating around prompt optimization.
commented.evidence_checked: Issue #2003, PR body, scenario code, README, generated sample reports, checks, and reviewer-run tests were inspected.
commented.problem_framing: The immediate goal is a runnable example, but the deeper goal is a regression contract that keeps prompt optimization from accepting hidden validation failures.
commented.approach: The PR builds a fake-mode example under `examples/evaluation/promptiter/regressionloop`, adds attribution data, scenario selection, reports, and reusable regression-loop helpers.
commented.implementation_derivation: A deterministic fake runner is appropriate because it lets CI and reviewers reproduce accepted, ineffective, overfit, and attribution scenarios without model credentials.
commented.solution_fit_assessment: The CLI scenario surface is the right user control, but its help text must match the implemented scenario list so users can discover all modes.
commented.alternative_designs: The example could be smaller with one scenario, or could rely on live models, but that would reduce audit coverage or reproducibility.
commented.tradeoffs: More committed fixtures and reports increase maintenance cost, but make the optimization behavior easy to verify in CI and review.
commented.design_assessment: The example structure is useful; the missing `attribution` help text is a small but real usability gap because it hides one supported scenario.
commented.modules: examples/evaluation/promptiter/regressionloop, evaluation/workflow/promptiter/regloop, sample eval sets and reports.
commented.api_surface: Adds example CLI flags such as `--mode`, `--scenario`, `--data-dir`, and `--output-dir`; no core runtime API is changed.
commented.change_inventory: Adds attribution datasets, scenario definitions, fake runner wiring, sample optimization reports, and tests for the regression loop.
commented.semantic_changes: Users can run deterministic scenario-specific PromptIter regression demos and inspect JSON/Markdown output.
commented.module_impact: Direct impact is example and evaluation workflow code; indirect impact is documentation expectations for PromptIter regression checks.
commented.cross_module_impact: PromptIter evaluation artifacts and example docs now depend on the scenario list being complete and consistent.
commented.behavior_impact: Expected before/after: users can run `--scenario=all` or individual scenarios; `--help` should expose every individual scenario.
commented.tests_docs: Reviewer reported package tests, coverage checks, fake-mode runs, and committed report diff checks passing.
commented.outcome: Commented that `--scenario` help omits the supported `attribution` scenario.
commented.risk: If unfixed, the attribution demo exists but is less discoverable from the command-line entry point.

commented.title: memory/gorm: GORM-backed memory.Service with shared DB injection
commented.author: sks

inline_comments.body:
`newGormMemoryService` opens the GORM handle here, but passes it as `WithDB`, so `memory/gorm.Close()` intentionally will not close it. Use a dialector so the service owns the client, or wrap the returned service to close the opened `sql.DB`.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
`newGormMemoryService` 在这里打开 GORM 连接后通过 `WithDB` 传入，因此 `memory/gorm.Close()` 按设计不会关闭它。请改用 dialector 让 service 持有连接，或包装返回的 service 来关闭这里打开的 `sql.DB`。
</details>

`-memory=gorm` now creates a GORM service, but `PrintMemoryInfo` still falls through to `In-memory`. Add a `MemoryGORM` case that prints GORM-specific, non-secret settings so the startup output matches the selected backend.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
`-memory=gorm` 现在会创建 GORM service，但 `PrintMemoryInfo` 仍会落到 `In-memory`。请增加 `MemoryGORM` 分支，输出 GORM 相关且不含敏感信息的配置，让启动信息和实际后端一致。
</details>

This README points users to `simple` / `auto` with `-memory=gorm`, but those actual discovery surfaces still omit GORM and its environment variables. Please update the flag help and the `simple` / `auto` READMEs so users can discover and configure the new backend from the real entrypoints.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
这里引导用户通过 `simple` / `auto` 使用 `-memory=gorm`，但真实入口的帮助信息和 README 仍缺少 GORM 及其环境变量。请同步更新 flag help 以及 `simple` / `auto` README，让用户能从真实入口发现并配置新后端。
</details>

After the standalone GORM app was removed, this README still describes removed flags, stale default tool behavior, and a `go test ./...` command for a README-only directory. Please merge the remaining reference material into the real example docs, or rewrite this file so it no longer reads like a runnable example.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
独立 GORM app 删除后，这个 README 仍在描述已移除的 flag、过期的默认工具行为，以及 README-only 目录下的 `go test ./...`。请把剩余参考内容合并到真实示例文档，或重写此文件，避免它继续表现成可运行示例。
</details>

commented.review_action: Submitted COMMENT review only; no approval, no LGTM, no merge. Review URL: https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2128#pullrequestreview-4661543241
commented.technical_background: The memory service abstraction stores long-term user memories behind a common `memory.Service` interface. GORM-backed services can either own a database connection they open themselves or receive a caller-owned `*gorm.DB`; that ownership choice determines whether `Close()` should release the underlying pool.
commented.problem: The PR adds a first-party `memory/gorm` backend and wires it into shared memory examples so users can use GORM-managed databases instead of exporting internal helper logic.
commented.necessity_assessment: confirmed as a design need: prior maintainer discussion asked for a contributed backend rather than exporting framework-internal memory helpers.
commented.evidence_checked: PR body, related #2109 discussion, GORM service options, service close behavior, example util code, README files, checks, and review comments were inspected.
commented.problem_framing: The user pain is integration with existing GORM-backed applications; the root contract is clear ownership of database lifecycle and clear discovery from the actual example entry points.
commented.approach: The PR introduces `memory/gorm`, service options for `WithDB`, `WithDialector`, table/init/soft-delete configuration, tests, and shared example selection via `-memory=gorm`.
commented.implementation_derivation: A new backend package is the right abstraction because it keeps storage-specific code behind the existing memory interface while preserving the public contract.
commented.solution_fit_assessment: The backend direction fits, but the examples currently open a DB and pass it as caller-owned, and the docs/help surfaces do not fully describe the new backend.
commented.alternative_designs: Alternatives include only documenting host-owned `WithDB`, only supporting `WithDialector`, or adding a small wrapper type in examples that owns the opened DB.
commented.tradeoffs: Supporting shared `*gorm.DB` is important for applications, but examples that open their own connection should either use owned dialectors or close the injected pool explicitly.
commented.design_assessment: The package design is broadly useful; the lifecycle and documentation gaps need fixing before the examples are safe and discoverable.
commented.modules: memory/gorm backend, examples/memory shared util, examples/memory README files, simple and auto memory examples.
commented.api_surface: Adds exported GORM memory service constructors/options such as `NewService`, `WithDB`, `WithDialector`, `WithSkipDBInit`, `WithSoftDelete`, tool options, and the `gorm` memory type in examples.
commented.change_inventory: Adds a GORM memory submodule, tests, shared example factory support, GORM reference docs, and example dependency updates.
commented.semantic_changes: Users can select `-memory=gorm`; GORM service may auto-migrate unless skip init is configured; injected DB handles are intentionally caller-owned.
commented.module_impact: Direct impact is memory backend storage and example setup; indirect impact is memory tool behavior and shutdown semantics in examples.
commented.cross_module_impact: Example runners, memory tools, GORM storage configuration, docs, and tests all need consistent lifecycle and option semantics.
commented.behavior_impact: Expected before/after: users can run simple/auto memory examples with a GORM backend and get matching startup info, configuration docs, and closed resources.
commented.tests_docs: CI checks were green. Review focused on service option semantics and docs because the issues are not caught by existing tests.
commented.outcome: Commented on DB lifecycle leakage, wrong startup backend label, missing simple/auto discovery docs, and stale GORM reference README content.
commented.risk: If unfixed, examples can leak database pools and users can receive misleading backend information or stale commands.

commented.title: examples/code_review_agent: add sandboxed code review agent example
commented.author: XnLemon

inline_comments.body:
Inside an active hunk, a real changed line can start with `--- ` or `+++ `, but these cases run before hunk-line parsing and reclassify that content as a file header/path. Handle hunk lines before file headers, or only accept headers outside active hunks, so valid diff content does not create phantom files or shift later findings.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
在已经进入 hunk 后，真实变更内容也可能以 `--- ` 或 `+++ ` 开头，但这里会先按文件头处理，导致这些内容被误判成文件路径。建议先处理 active hunk 内的 diff 行，或只在 hunk 外接受文件头，避免合法 diff 内容制造假文件或影响后续 finding 的行号归属。
</details>

commented.review_action: Submitted COMMENT review only; no approval, no LGTM, no merge. Review URL: https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2121#pullrequestreview-4661593247
commented.technical_background: A code-review example that parses unified diffs depends on precise file and hunk parsing. In unified diff format, `---` and `+++` mark file headers only outside hunks; inside a hunk, changed content can also begin with those characters.
commented.problem: The PR adds a sandboxed code review agent example with skill files, diff parsing, fake runtime, persistence, reports, and safety controls.
commented.necessity_assessment: confirmed as a feature need: issue #2004 asks for a sandboxed code-review agent example and the base tree has no such example.
commented.evidence_checked: Issue #2004, PR body, diff parser, fixtures, generated reports, checks, and reviewer-run example commands/tests were inspected.
commented.problem_framing: The core product need is an auditable example; the parser must preserve exact changed file and line mapping or later review findings become unreliable.
commented.approach: The PR introduces an isolated example with internal packages for diff parsing, rules, redaction, sandbox execution, report generation, and durable storage.
commented.implementation_derivation: Keeping this as an example package is reasonable because it demonstrates composition of existing framework tools without changing core runtime APIs.
commented.solution_fit_assessment: The architecture fits an example, but the diff parser needs a format-correct hunk/file-header boundary to avoid wrong file attribution.
commented.alternative_designs: Alternatives include using an existing diff parser library, parsing hunk lines before headers only when inside a hunk, or keeping a stricter state machine.
commented.tradeoffs: A small custom parser is easy to read but must cover ambiguous-looking hunk content; a library would reduce edge cases but add dependency weight.
commented.design_assessment: The example is useful, with a concrete parser correctness bug that can misplace later findings.
commented.modules: examples/code_review_agent/internal/diffparse plus downstream rules/reporting that consume parsed file and line metadata.
commented.api_surface: Adds example CLI and internal example packages; no exported core framework API changes.
commented.change_inventory: Adds README/design docs, fixtures, parser/rules/redaction/report/sandbox/store packages, fake runtime, and tests.
commented.semantic_changes: Users can run a local code-review example on fixtures, diff files, file lists, or repositories and receive findings/reports.
commented.module_impact: Direct impact is the example parser; indirect impact is any rule or report that relies on parsed hunk line numbers.
commented.cross_module_impact: Rules, redaction, sandbox, and report packages depend on the parser preserving correct file/hunk identity.
commented.behavior_impact: Expected before/after: legal hunk content starting with `--- ` or `+++ ` should remain a changed line, not become a new file header.
commented.tests_docs: Reviewer reported `go test -count=1 ./...` and several fake-mode example runs passing; the parser edge case still needs a regression test.
commented.outcome: Commented on the active-hunk parser ordering bug.
commented.risk: If unfixed, valid diffs can create phantom files or shift finding locations.

commented.title: 实现自动代码评审Agent全套能力
commented.author: xiaoyulox

inline_comments.body:
This advertises E2B/container sandbox selection, but both selected backends still return `not implemented`, so a production configuration with those env vars cannot run sandbox checks. Wire these paths to the existing `codeexecutor/e2b` or `codeexecutor/container` workspace runtimes, or narrow the contract to an explicit unsafe-local prototype until isolated backends are implemented.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
这里声明会选择 E2B/container 沙箱，但两个被选中的后端仍然只返回 `not implemented`，所以配置了这些环境变量的生产运行无法执行沙箱检查。请把这些路径接到现有的 `codeexecutor/e2b` 或 `codeexecutor/container` workspace runtime，或者在真正实现隔离后端前，把契约收窄为显式 unsafe-local 原型。
</details>

The default `--repo-path` flow only diffs `merge-base..HEAD`, so staged or uncommitted local changes are omitted. Since the issue requires reviewing a local changed directory, include the working-tree/index diff or split this into an explicit committed-PR mode and local-workspace mode with tests.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
默认 `--repo-path` 流程只比较 `merge-base..HEAD`，因此会漏掉 staged 或未提交的本地变更。由于 issue 要求支持本地变更目录，请把 working tree/index 的 diff 也纳入，或者明确拆成“已提交 PR 模式”和“本地工作区模式”并补测试。
</details>

Dry-run currently skips the entire static-analysis/sandbox block, even though the docs promise dry-run still runs static analysis and rule matching without LLM calls. Keep deterministic sandbox/static checks enabled in dry-run, or split this into a separate no-sandbox mode and update the docs/tests.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
当前 dry-run 会跳过整个静态分析/沙箱块，但文档承诺 dry-run 仍会在不调用 LLM 的情况下运行静态分析和规则匹配。请在 dry-run 中保留确定性的沙箱/静态检查，或者拆出单独的 no-sandbox 模式并同步更新文档和测试。
</details>

`GenerateReport` only writes `report.md`, but the PR/docs contract requires `review_report.json` and `review_report.md`. Emit both documented artifacts with the structured findings, metrics, sandbox, and permission data, and add a test that verifies the expected filenames and contents.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
`GenerateReport` 目前只写 `report.md`，但 PR/文档契约要求输出 `review_report.json` 和 `review_report.md`。请输出这两个文档化产物，并包含结构化 findings、metrics、sandbox、permission 数据，同时补测试校验文件名和内容。
</details>

commented.review_action: Submitted COMMENT review only; no approval, no LGTM, no merge. Review URL: https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2151#pullrequestreview-4661593245. Contributor-program/manual-selection context still requires explicit maintainer decision; these comments do not imply merge readiness.
commented.technical_background: A code-review agent example combines repository diff collection, deterministic static checks, sandbox execution, permissions, and report artifacts. The command-line contract matters because users rely on documented flags and output filenames to validate the whole review chain.
commented.problem: The PR adds a full code review agent prototype with skill, sandbox selection, database persistence, reports, and fake/no-key operation.

commented.evidence_checked: Issue #2004, PR body, latest patch, sandbox constructors, repo diff collection, dry-run flow, report generation, checks, and review comments were inspected.
commented.problem_framing: The stated goal is a complete runnable review chain; the deeper requirement is that each advertised mode actually exercises the chain it claims to validate.
commented.approach: The PR builds an example CLI with diff/repo inputs, sandbox abstraction, dry-run mode, permission and storage layers, and Markdown reporting.
commented.implementation_derivation: An isolated example is a reasonable place to prototype the workflow, but advertised production sandbox and artifact contracts need to be real or explicitly out of scope.
commented.solution_fit_assessment: The selected control surfaces are understandable, but several flags and outputs currently do less than the docs promise.
commented.alternative_designs: Alternatives include narrowing the PR to an unsafe-local prototype, wiring existing `codeexecutor` backends now, or splitting local-workspace and committed-PR modes.
commented.tradeoffs: A broad example demonstrates integration value, but broad scope raises the bar for accurate docs, real sandbox backends, and end-to-end artifact tests.
commented.design_assessment: The prototype has useful structure but is not yet aligned with its documented contract; the comments identify concrete contract gaps.
commented.modules: examples/code_review_agent main CLI, sandbox package, output reporting package, skill docs, storage and static-analysis flow.
commented.api_surface: Adds example flags such as `--repo-path`, `--dry-run`, sandbox env selection, report outputs, and internal example package APIs.

commented.module_impact: Direct impact is the example; indirect impact is contributor expectations for sandboxed review-agent patterns.
commented.cross_module_impact: The example references existing codeexecutor concepts, permission policy, storage, and report consumers even though it is isolated from core packages.
commented.behavior_impact: Expected before/after: documented sandbox modes should run, local workspace changes should be included, dry-run should still exercise static checks, and documented JSON/Markdown reports should exist.
commented.tests_docs: CI was green. Review relied on source inspection because the contract gaps are around mode behavior and artifact names.
commented.outcome: Commented on unimplemented E2B/container backends, missing working-tree diff, dry-run skipping static/sandbox checks, and report filenames/artifacts not matching docs.
commented.risk: If unfixed, users can run a green example that does not exercise the advertised safety, local review, or reporting behavior.

commented.title: 构建 Evaluation + Optimization 自动回归闭环
commented.author: GodBlf

inline_comments.body:
Missing validation cases never fail the gate: `ComputeDeltas` emits `TransitionMissing`, but `EvaluateGate` only checks score, hard fails, critical regressions, and budgets, so a candidate can be accepted after skipping baseline validation cases. Add a gate rule that rejects missing validation cases before `Accepted` is set, and cover baseline-only/candidate-only deltas in tests.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
缺失的验证用例不会让 gate 失败：`ComputeDeltas` 会产生 `TransitionMissing`，但 `EvaluateGate` 只检查分数、hard fail、关键回归和预算，所以候选 prompt 即使跳过了 baseline 验证用例也可能被接受。请在设置 `Accepted` 前增加缺失验证用例的拒绝规则，并补充 baseline-only / candidate-only delta 测试。
</details>

`MarkdownReport` inserts candidate prompts into a single inline code span without escaping, so prompts containing backticks, pipes, or newlines can corrupt `optimization_report.md` and make the required audit report hard to read. Render prompts and other dynamic report text through Markdown escaping or fenced blocks, and add a report test with those characters.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
`MarkdownReport` 会把候选 prompt 原样写进单行内联代码块，包含反引号、管道符或换行的真实 prompt 会破坏 `optimization_report.md`，导致必须交付的人类可读审计报告难以阅读。请对 prompt 和其他动态报告文本做 Markdown 转义，或改用代码块渲染，并补充覆盖这些字符的报告测试。
</details>

commented.review_action: Submitted COMMENT review only; no approval, no LGTM, no merge. Review URL: https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2152#pullrequestreview-4661593240
commented.technical_background: Evaluation regression gates compare baseline and candidate results case by case. A gate must treat missing validation cases as a failure because a candidate can otherwise appear safer by not evaluating a difficult case.
commented.problem: The PR builds an evaluation and optimization regression loop with failure attribution, baseline/candidate deltas, release gates, and audit reports.
commented.necessity_assessment: confirmed as a feature need from issue #2003; the base tree lacks this regression-loop package and example.
commented.evidence_checked: Issue #2003, latest diff, gate logic, report renderer, checks, and reviewer-run package/example tests were inspected.
commented.problem_framing: The immediate need is a reproducible loop; the root correctness issue is making the gate reject hidden regressions and making reports robust for arbitrary prompt text.
commented.approach: The PR adds a reusable regression-loop package, delta computation, gate policies, JSON/Markdown reporting, and an example with fake runner data.
commented.implementation_derivation: A package-level regression loop is a reasonable implementation point because it can be reused by examples and future prompt surfaces.
commented.solution_fit_assessment: The structure fits the need, but the gate omits missing-case failure handling and the Markdown report does not escape dynamic prompt text.
commented.alternative_designs: Alternatives include failing missing cases during delta computation, validating eval-set identity before scoring, or rendering all prompt text in fenced blocks.
commented.tradeoffs: Strict missing-case rejection may fail more runs early, but it prevents false acceptance; fenced report text is less compact but safer for real prompts.
commented.design_assessment: The package is directionally useful, with two correctness/reporting gaps that should be fixed before relying on its acceptance result.
commented.modules: evaluation/workflow/promptiter/regressionloop gate, delta, report, pipeline, and examples/evaluation promptiter regression loop.
commented.api_surface: Adds regression-loop package types, config JSON fields, output report paths, and example CLI configuration; no existing core API is removed.
commented.change_inventory: Adds gate policy evaluation, attribution, delta computation, pipeline orchestration, JSON/Markdown report generation, tests, and example fixtures.
commented.semantic_changes: Users can run a deterministic optimization loop and inspect accepted/rejected candidates, deltas, and reports.
commented.module_impact: Direct impact is evaluation/promptiter workflow code; indirect impact is any future automation that trusts the gate decision.
commented.cross_module_impact: PromptIter engine adapters, examples, report consumers, and release gating all depend on complete validation case coverage.
commented.behavior_impact: Expected before/after: missing validation cases should fail the gate, and real prompt text should not break Markdown reports.
commented.tests_docs: Reviewer reported workflow and example tests passing; additional gate/report edge tests are needed.
commented.outcome: Commented on missing-case acceptance and unescaped Markdown prompt rendering.
commented.risk: If unfixed, the regression loop can accept incomplete validation results or produce broken audit reports.

commented.title: tool/safety: add pre-execution safety scanner
commented.author: XnLemon

inline_comments.body:
`audit_failure_mode` is accepted in the policy file, but the documented `NewPermissionPolicy(scanner)` path always resets the adapter to best-effort auditing. Initialize the permission policy from `*DefaultScanner`'s policy audit mode, while keeping `WithAuditFailureMode` as an override, and cover the strict-mode audit failure path without passing the option explicitly.

<details><summary>中文</summary>
策略文件会接受 `audit_failure_mode`，但文档里的 `NewPermissionPolicy(scanner)` 接入路径总是把适配器重置成 best-effort 审计。请从 `*DefaultScanner` 的 policy 中初始化 permission policy 的 audit mode，同时保留 `WithAuditFailureMode` 作为显式覆盖，并补一个不传该 option 时 strict 审计失败会生效的回归测试。
</details>

commented.review_action: Submitted COMMENT review only; no approval, no LGTM, no merge. Review URL: https://github.com/trpc-group/trpc-agent-go/pull/2136#pullrequestreview-4661593234
commented.technical_background: Tool permission policies sit between a tool call and execution. A scanner policy may allow or deny commands, and audit mode decides whether failure to record an audit event should fail closed or remain best-effort.
commented.problem: The PR adds a tool execution safety guard with policy loading, scanner decisions, permission-policy integration, audit records, telemetry, examples, and docs.
commented.necessity_assessment: confirmed as a feature need from issue #2002; the base tree has no `tool/safety` package.
commented.evidence_checked: Issue #2002, policy loader, permission adapter, docs/example integration path, checks, and reviewer-run tests/example were inspected.
commented.problem_framing: The user pain is safer tool execution; the root contract is that a policy file must actually control the adapter used at execution time.
commented.approach: The PR introduces `tool/safety` scanner policy, default rules, audit failure modes, permission adapter, examples, and tests.
commented.implementation_derivation: A scanner plus permission adapter is the right layer because it can inspect tool requests before execution and centralize audit behavior.
commented.solution_fit_assessment: The design fits, but the adapter currently ignores `audit_failure_mode` from the loaded scanner policy unless the caller separately passes an option.
commented.alternative_designs: Alternatives include making `NewPermissionPolicy` accept a full `Policy`, exposing scanner policy accessors, or requiring explicit adapter options and removing the file-field promise.
commented.tradeoffs: Propagating policy audit mode keeps YAML/JSON behavior intuitive; explicit options still need to override for callers that intentionally differ.
commented.design_assessment: The package direction is sound; this one wiring gap weakens strict audit guarantees and should be fixed with a regression test.
commented.modules: tool/safety policy, scanner, permission adapter, audit writer, docs and example module.
commented.api_surface: Adds `Policy`, `AuditFailureMode`, scanner constructors, `NewPermissionPolicy`, `WithAuditFailureMode`, policy file fields, and example integration APIs.
commented.change_inventory: Adds policy parsing/validation, scanner rules, permission adapter, audit failure handling, telemetry/docs/examples, and tests.
commented.semantic_changes: Users can configure command safety and audit handling before tool execution; strict audit mode should fail closed when audit writes fail.
commented.module_impact: Direct impact is tool safety checks; indirect impact is codeexec or hostexec tool calls that adopt the permission adapter.
commented.cross_module_impact: Policy files, scanner state, permission policy, audit writer, and tool execution wrappers must agree on audit semantics.
commented.behavior_impact: Expected before/after: `audit_failure_mode: strict` in policy should make the documented adapter path fail closed on audit write failure.
commented.tests_docs: Reviewer reported `go test ./tool/safety ./tool/codeexec ./internal/shellsafe`, example tests, example run, and redaction grep passing.
commented.outcome: Commented that `NewPermissionPolicy(scanner)` resets audit mode to best-effort instead of inheriting scanner policy strict mode.
commented.risk: If unfixed, users can configure strict audit in YAML/JSON and still get fail-open behavior through the documented path.

timeline.detail: Ran git pull --ff-only in the MyCR workspace; it was already up to date.
Collected 59 open PRs and per-PR effective checks with gh/REST.
Submitted COMMENT reviews on #2141, #2157, #2128, #2121, #2151, #2152, and #2136; submitted no approval and no merge.
#2158 and #2159 were checked deeply and had no new blocking finding, but no LGTM was submitted.

skill_evolution.note: Comment-only runs need first-class reviewed-clean deferred cards so clean reviews do not look like missing coverage.
gh pr checks snapshots should be refreshed near report time for PRs that were pending during the initial sweep.
